assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a Version Impact Table with 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 marked 'YES' (h2 0.4.5, below fix threshold 0.4.8) and 2.2.0 through 2.2.4 all marked 'NO' (h2 >= 0.4.8). Lock file evidence is referenced via 'git show <tag>:Cargo.lock' at pinned commits (v0.3.8, v0.3.12, v0.4.5, etc.). The Stream-Level Summary explicitly states '2.1.x: YES' and '2.2.x: NO'.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows the proposed correction as 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]', changing from 'Current: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.2.0]'. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly removed ('Removed: RHTPA 2.2.0 -- lock file analysis confirms h2 0.4.8 (fixed)... this version is NOT affected'). Only 2.1.x versions remain in the corrected list.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates exactly two tasks, both scoped to the 2.1.x stream: Task 1 targets 'release/0.3.z' (the 2.1.x upstream branch) and Task 2 targets 'rhtpa-release.0.3.z' (the 2.1.x downstream repo). A dedicated section 'Why No Remediation for 2.2.x' states 'all 2.2.x versions ship h2 >= 0.4.8 (the fix threshold)... the 2.2.x stream does not require any remediation.' No tasks are created for the 2.2.x stream.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'The issue summary... contains no stream suffix in brackets. The issue is therefore unscoped -- it covers all configured version streams.' It lists both 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams and states 'All streams will be analyzed in Step 2.' The version-impact.md table includes all 7 versions across both streams (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4), confirming all streams were checked.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains a dedicated section 'Why No Cross-Stream Impact Notice' stating: 'The issue TC-8004 is unscoped... An unscoped issue covers all streams by definition. Cross-stream impact notices (Case B) are only generated for scoped issues where the analysis reveals impact on streams outside the issue's scope. Since an unscoped issue has no scope boundary, there is no "outside" to report on.' No cross-stream impact notice artifact exists in the outputs directory.
